# Audrique

> Open-source automated voice testing framework for enterprise contact centers.

## What it does

Audrique is the first open-source tool that tests across telephony, CRM UI, and backend records simultaneously. It orchestrates real voice calls through Amazon Connect and Twilio, verifies agent-side behavior in Salesforce Service Cloud Voice via Playwright, and validates backend records — all in a single declarative test scenario.

No mocks, no stubs — real phone calls, real CRM verification, real results.

## Who it's for

- QA engineers testing contact center voice workflows
- Salesforce admins validating Service Cloud Voice + Amazon Connect integrations
- DevOps teams building CI/CD pipelines for telephony systems
- Contact center platform teams doing regression testing after routing changes

## Key capabilities

- Visual scenario builder (Scenario Studio) — build voice tests in a browser, no code required
- Three parallel recording streams: Agent browser, CCP dialer, Supervisor console
- IVR navigation with speech detection and DTMF digit routing
- Supervisor Command Center monitoring (Queue Backlog + In-Progress Work)
- Omni-Channel agent state verification (login, provider sync, call acceptance)
- VoiceCall record screen pop assertion
- Timeline-synced video evidence generation via FFmpeg
- Session management with Connect GetFederationToken API (cookie injection, no SAML)
- Docker support with Vault integration for secrets management
- Runs from UI (SSE streaming) or CLI

## Technology stack

- Node.js 18+
- Playwright (browser automation)
- Amazon Connect CCP (telephony)
- Twilio (outbound dialing)
- Salesforce Lightning (CRM verification)
- FFmpeg (video evidence)
- Docker + HashiCorp Vault (deployment)

## Architecture

A test scenario coordinates three parallel streams:

1. **Agent stream** — Playwright controls a Salesforce Lightning browser session. Logs into Omni-Channel, waits for incoming call, accepts it, verifies VoiceCall screen pop.
2. **CCP stream** — Playwright controls an Amazon Connect CCP session. Places outbound call via Twilio, navigates IVR with DTMF, monitors call state.
3. **Supervisor stream** — Playwright opens Salesforce Command Center. Monitors Queue Backlog and In-Progress Work tabs for call routing verification.

All three streams record video simultaneously. Timeline timestamps synchronize the recordings for evidence generation.

## Scenario definition

Tests are defined as JSON scenarios with steps:
- preflight (Omni-Channel login + provider sync)
- start_supervisor (open Command Center, establish baseline counts)
- trigger_call (CCP outbound dial with optional IVR navigation)
- detect_incoming (wait for Omni-Channel offer)
- accept_call (click accept on agent widget)
- verify_screen_pop (assert VoiceCall record created)

## Installation

```bash
git clone https://github.com/snehalsurti12/audrique.git
cd audrique
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

## Usage

```bash
# Start Scenario Studio (visual builder)
npm run studio

# Run a test suite
node scripts/run-instance-e2e-suite.mjs --suite scenarios/e2e/my-suite.json

# Run via Docker with Vault
docker compose run --rm audrique node scripts/run-instance-e2e-suite.mjs --suite scenarios/e2e/my-suite.json
```
